Colombia.status: Already qualified, top of group on points

Portugal.status: Already qualified, massive +5 GD edge — market actually favors Portugal today over Colombia

COL.CRITICAL: Colombia's offside count is extreme and consistent in direction (3, then 7) — a genuinely aggressive, high-line-breaking attacking team, not noise.
COL.PRIOR_LESSON: UZB-COL GD1 showed Uzbekistan out-SOT'd Colombia in the 2H despite losing 1-3 — Colombia 'manages' a lead in the 2H rather than piling on. This dynamic requires COLOMBIA TO BE COMFORTABLY AHEAD to kick in; today Colombia is actually the underdog (24.85%), so this pattern is less likely to apply the same way.

POR.PRIOR_LESSON_RAMOS: POR-UZB: Ramos did NOT score in the 5-0 win; main books didn't list him and a niche book's high price was stale/wrong — 'main book absence = probability <35%' was the lesson. TODAY Ramos IS present in the main Smarkets market at a high price (0.74 raw) — the absence-signal condition does NOT apply this time, so the discount rule should not be blindly reused; treat this as a genuine market read with modest trim for general player-prop variance.

context_flags: MARKET_FAVORS_POR_DESPITE_COL_TOPPING_TABLE: Colombia has more points (6 vs 4) but Portugal is the clear market favorite (51.0% vs 24.9%) — reflects Portugal's superior quality and +5 GD vs Colombia's grittier 1-0/3-1 wins.
COL_OFFSIDE_EXTREME: 3 then 7 offsides across 2 matches — among the most extreme, consistent attacking-line signals in the whole dataset. Trust near-raw per WINNING TRAIL.

RAMOS_LESSON_DOES_NOT_DIRECTLY_TRANSFER: the POR-UZB discount on Ramos was specifically about market absence as a signal of bench role; he's liquid and highly priced here, so apply only a small generic trim, not the blowout-discount logic.

questions.text: Will Colombia be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.reasoning: Colombia offsides: 3 (vs UZB) and 7 (vs CDR). λ=5.0 raw Poisson gives P(≥2)=0.96. Trimmed only slightly for the chance the 7-offside game was partly opponent-specific (CDR's exploitable deep line) — still extremely high confidence.

questions.text: In the second half, will Colombia have more shots on target than Portugal?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Skellam off full-match averages (COL λ_2H=3.25, POR λ_2H=2.5) gives 0.54 raw. Colombia's known 'ease off once ahead' 2H pattern (UZB-COL GD1) doesn't apply cleanly here since Colombia is the underdog today, not a team nursing a lead — nets out close to the raw calculation with a small trim for Portugal's overall quality edge.

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Penalty Yes=26.59%, Any sent off Yes=13.36%. P(OR)=1-(0.7341×0.8664)=0.3640.

questions.text: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Yes/Over=44.07%. Trust it — both teams have scored freely this tournament (Colombia 4 goals, Portugal 6 goals across 2 matches each).
Smarkets FTR: Colombia 24.85%. Direct tier-1 market — trust it over Colombia's superior points total, since the market already reflects Portugal's quality and goal-difference edge.

questions.text: Will Colombia have 4 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Colombia Over3.5 SOT = 48.44%. Trust it.

questions.text: Will Portugal have 4 or more shots on target?
questions.reasoning: Market only lists the 4.5 threshold (52.32%→ wait 54.24% Over4.5); backing out the implied lambda (4.90) and computing P(≥4) directly gives 0.72.

questions.text: Will there be 5 or more total corner kicks in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Combined full-match corner average (COL 4.5 + POR 4.0 = 8.5), half-split λ≈4.25 → Poisson P(≥5)=0.42. No specific market exists for this exact split; ESPN-based estimate used directly since neither team's corner history this tournament includes the kind of stale-history pitfall we corrected for in CRO-GHA (both teams' corner counts have been fairly stable, not hiding a single outlier game).

questions.text: Will Luis Díaz score a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.reasoning: Illiquid full-match offer (0.2564, adjusted ×0.945=0.242). Note this question asks specifically about SCORING (not score-or-assist, which is a higher-probability superset) — trust the direct goalscorer market rather than reusing the broader score-or-assist number from COL-CDR (0.48 there was for a different, easier-to-clear bar).

questions.text: Will Gonçalo Ramos have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.reasoning: Illiquid full-match offer (0.7407, adjusted ×0.945=0.700). Unlike POR-UZB (where Ramos was absent from main books, a signal he'd barely play), he's liquid and high-priced here — the absence-discount lesson doesn't apply. Small generic trim for standard player-prop variance.

strongest_picks: Q1: Colombia 2+ offsides = 0.85 (HIGHEST CONFIDENCE — among the most extreme, consistent attacking-line signals in the whole dataset)
Q7: Portugal 4+ SOT = 0.72 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — market-derived lambda)
Q5: Colombia wins = 0.25 (HIGH CONFIDENCE — direct market, correctly overrides the points-table narrative)
